semanticCore: Humbling oneself, submission, poor, miserable, yield, or adapt
quranicRelevance: Not weakening or submitting (Istakanoo - 'wa-ma istakanoo', 'fama istakanoo').

keyDerivations.meaning: To humble oneself, submit, yield, show weakness, surrender, or be subdued

figurativeOrRareUsages: فَمَا وَهَنُوا لِمَا أَصَابَهُمْ فِي سَبِيلِ اللَّهِ وَمَا ضَعُفُوا وَمَا اسْتَكَانُوا (3:146): And they did not lose heart for what befell them in the way of God, nor did they weaken or submit (battlefield resilience).
discardedNoise: Poverty houses (Meskeen) - belonging to s-k-n, though conceptually overlapping in Arabic dictionaries.
summaryEn: The root represents submissive yielding (Istikanah), praising the battlefield resilience of past prophets' companions who refused to submit or show weakness to enemies.
summaryAr: يمثل هذا الجذر الخضوع والاستسلام والذل أمام العدو أو الخالق (استكانة)، مادحاً صمود أصحاب الأنبياء الذين لم يهنوا ولم يستكينوا للطغاة.
rawText:
اِسْتَكَانَ 10 : see اِسْتَكَنَ in art. سكن: and see art. كون.
كَيْنٌ (also written كَيْنَةٌ, K, voce زَرْنَبٌ): see بَظْرٌ.
